questions.text:
The encoding technique used to transmit the signal in giga ethernet technology over  fiber optic medium is

  1. Differential Manchester encoding
  2. Non return to zero
  3. $\text{4B/5B}$ encoding
  4. $\text{8B/10B}$ encoding

Which of the following is an unsupervised neural network?

  1. RBS
  2. Hopfield
  3. Back propagation
  4. Kohonen

In compiler terminology reduction in strength means

  1. Replacing run time computation by compile time computation
  2. Removing loop invariant computation
  3. Removing common subexpressions
  4. replacing a costly operation by a relatively cheaper one

The following table shows the processes in the ready queue and time required for each process for completing its job.

Process Time
$P_1$ 10
$P_2$ 5
$P_3$ 20
$P_4$ 8
$P_5$ 15

If round-robin scheduling with 5 ms is used what is the average waiting time of the processes in the queue?

  1. 27 ms
  2. 26.2 ms
  3. 27.5 ms
  4. 27.2 ms

$\textsf{MOV [BX], AL}$  type of data addressing is called?

  1. register
  2. immediate
  3. register indirect
  4. register relative

Evaluate $\text{(X xor Y) xor Y}?$

  1. All $\text{1's}$
  2. All $\text{0's}$
  3. $\text{X}$
  4. $\text{Y}$

Which of the following is true about $z$-buffer algorithm?

  1. It is a depth sort algorithm
  2. No limitation on total number of objects
  3. Comparisons of objects is done
  4. $z$-buffer is initialized to background colour at start of algorithm

What is the decimal value of the floating-point number $C1D00000$ (hexadecimal notation)? (Assume $32$-bit, single precision floating point $\text{IEEE}$ representation)

  1. $28$
  2. $-15$
  3. $-26$
  4. $-28$

What is the raw throughput of USB $2.0$ technology?

  1. $480$ Mbps
  2. $400$ Mbps
  3. $200$ Mbps
  4. $12$ Mbps

Below is the precedence graph for a set of tasks to be executed on a parallel processing system $S$.

What is the efficiency of this precedence graph on $S$ if each of the tasks $T_1, \dots, T_8$ takes the same time and the system $S$ has five processors?

  1. $25\%$
  2. $40\%$
  3. $50\%$
  4. $90\%$

How many distinct binary search trees can be created out of $4$ distinct keys?

  1. $5$
  2. $14$
  3. $24$
  4. $35$

The network protocol which is used to get MAC address of a node by providing IP address is

  1. SMTP
  2. ARP
  3. RIP
  4. BOOTP

Which of the following statements about peephole optimization is False?

  1. It is applied to a small part of the code
  2. It can be used to optimize intermediate code
  3. To get the best out of this, it has to be applied repeatedly
  4. It can be applied to the portion of the code that is not contiguous

Which one of the following in place sorting algorithms needs the minimum number of swaps?

  1. Quick sort
  2. Insertion sort
  3. Selection sort
  4. Heap sort

What is the equivalent serial schedule for the following transactions?

Transaction

T1T2T3
  R(Y)
  R(Z)
R(X)  
W(X)  
  W(Y)
  W(Z)
 W(Z) 
R(Y)  
W(Y)  
 R(Y) 
 W(Y) 
 R(X) 
 W(X) 

 

  1. $\text{T}_1 - \text{T}_2 - \text{T}_3$
  2. $\text{T}_3 - \text{T}_1 - \text{T}_2$
  3. $\text{T}_2 - \text{T}_1 - \text{T}_3$
  4. $\text{T}_1 - \text{T}_3 - \text{T}_2$

Consider a direct mapped cache with $64$ blocks and a block size of $16$ bytes. To what block number does the byte address $1206$ map to

  1. does not map
  2. $6$
  3. $11$
  4. $54$

A context model of a software system can be shown by drawing a

  1. LEVEL-0 DFD
  2. LEVEL-1 DFD
  3. LEVEL-2 DFD
  4. LEVEL-3 DFD

An example of poly-alphabetic substitution  is

  1. P-box
  2. S-box
  3. Caesar cipher
  4. Vigenere cipher

If node A has three siblings and B is parent of A, what is the degree of A?

  1. 0
  2. 3
  3. 4
  4. 5

The $\textsf{IEEE}$ standard for WiMax technology is

  1. $\textsf{IEEE 802.16}$
  2. $\textsf{IEEE 802.36}$
  3. $\textsf{IEEE 812.16}$
  4. $\textsf{IEEE 806.16}$

Which type of DBMS provides support for maintaining several versions of the same entity?

  1. Relational Database Management System
  2. Hierarchical
  3. Object Oriented Database Management System
  4. Network

A system is having $8\;\text{M}$ bytes of video memory for bit-mapped graphics with $64$-bit colour. What is the maximum resolution it can support?

  1. $800 \times 600$
  2. $1024 \times 768$
  3. $1280 \times 1024$
  4. $1920 \times 1440$

What is the meaning of $\overline{\textsf{RD}}$ signal in Intel $\textsf{8151A}?$

  1. Read (when it is low)
  2. Read (when it is high)
  3. Write (when it is low)
  4. Read and Write (when it is high)

If the page size in a 32-bit machine is 4K bytes then the size of page table is

  1. 1 M bytes
  2. 2 M bytes
  3. 4 M bytes
  4. 4 K bytes

A processor takes $12$ cycles to complete an instruction I. The corresponding pipelined processor uses $6$ stages with the execution times of $3, 2, 5, 4, 6$ and $2$ cycles respectively. What is the asymptotic speedup assuming that a very large number of instructions are to be executed?

  1. $1.83$
  2. $2$
  3. $3$
  4. $6$

The in-order traversal of a tree resulted in FBGADCE. Then the pre-order traversal of that tree would result in

  1. FGBDECA
  2. ABFGCDE
  3. BFGCDEA
  4. AFGBDEC

Which one of the following is true?

  1. $R \cap S = ( R \cup S) - [(R-S) \cup (S-R)]$
  2. $R \cup S = ( R \cap S) - [(R-S) \cup (S-R)]$
  3. $R \cap S = ( R \cup S) - [(R-S) \cap (S-R)]$
  4. $R \cap S = ( R \cup S) \cup (R-S)$

The above figure represents which one of the following UML diagram for a single send session of an online chat system?

  1. Package diagram
  2. Activity diagram
  3. Class diagram
  4. Sequence diagram

Which normal form is based on the concept of 'full functional dependency' is

  1. First Normal Form
  2. Second  Normal Form
  3. Third Normal Form
  4. Fourth Normal Form

In Boolean algebra, rule $\left ( \text{X + Y} \right )\left ( \text{X + Z} \right ) =$

  1. $\text{Y + XZ}$
  2. $\text{X + YZ}$
  3. $\text{XY + Z}$
  4. $\text{XZ + Y}$

How many $3$-to-$8$ line decoders with an enable input are needed to construct a $6$-to-$64$ line decoder without using any other logic gates?

  1. $7$
  2. $8$
  3. $9$
  4. $10$

In which layer of  network architecture, the secured socket layer (SSL) is used?

  1. physical layer
  2. session layer
  3. application layer
  4. presentation layer

What is the bit rate of a video terminal unit with $80$ characters/line, $8$ $\text{bits/character}$ and horizontal sweep time of $100$ $\text{µs}$ (including $20$ $\text{µs}$ of retrace time)?

  1. $8$ $\text{Mbps}$
  2. $6.4$ $\text{Mbps}$
  3. $0.8$ $\text{Mbps}$
  4. $0.64$ $\text{Mbps}$

Black Box Software Testing method focuses on the

  1. Boundary condition of the software
  2. Control structure of the software
  3. Functional requirement of the software
  4. Independent paths of the software

How many edges are there in a forest with $v$ vertices and $k$ components?

  1. $(v+1) - k$
  2. $(v+1)/2 - k$
  3. $v - k$
  4. $v + k$

If $\text{A}$ and $\text{B}$ are square matrices with same order and $\text{A}$ is symmetric, then $\text{B}^{\text{T}}\text{AB}$ is

  1. Skew symmetric
  2. Symmetric
  3. Orthogonal
  4. Idempotent

Find the memory address of the next instruction executed by the microprocessor $(8086),$ when operated in real mode for $\textsf{CS=1000}$ and $\textsf{IP=E000}$

  1. $\textsf{10E00}$
  2. $\textsf{1E000}$
  3. $\textsf{F000}$
  4. $\textsf{1000E}$

A fast wide SCSI-II disk drive spins at 7200 RPM, has a sector size of 512 bytes, and holds 160 sectors per track. Estimate the sustained transfer rate of this drive

  1. 576000 Kilobytes / sec
  2. 9600 Kilobytes / sec
  3. 4800 Kilobytes / sec
  4. 19200 Kilobytes / sec

Two control signals in microprocessor which are related to Direct Memory Access (DMA) are

  1. $\textsf{INTR & INTA}$
  2. $\textsf{RD & WR}$
  3. $\textsf{S0 & S1}$
  4. $\textsf{HOLD & HLDA}$

Consider the following pseudocode

x:=1;
i:=1;
while ( x <= 500)
begin
x:=2^x;
i:=i+1;
end

What is the value of $\textsf{i}$ at the end of the pseudocode?

  1. $4$
  2. $5$
  3. $6$
  4. $7$

If a microcomputer operates at $5$ MHz with an $8$-bit bus and a newer version operates at $20$ MHz with a $32$-bit bus, the maximum speed-up possible approximately will be

  1. $2$
  2. $4$
  3. $8$
  4. $16$

The search concept used in associative memory is

  1. Parallel search
  2. Sequential search
  3. Binary search
  4. Selection search

Which variable does not drive a terminal string in grammar?

  • $S \rightarrow AB$
  • $A \rightarrow a$
  • $B \rightarrow b$
  • $B \rightarrow C$
  1. A
  2. B
  3. C
  4. S

In Java, after executing the following code what are the values of x, y and z?

int x,y=10; z=12;

x=y++ + z++;

  1. x=22, y=10, z=12
  2. x=24, y=10, z=12
  3. x=24, y=11, z=13
  4. x=22, y=11, z=13

The broadcast address for IP network $172.16.0.0$ with subnet mask $255.255.0.0$ is

  1. $172.16.0.255$
  2. $172.16.255.255$
  3. $255.255.255.255$
  4. $172.255.255.255$

Which $\textsf{RAID}$ level gives block level striping with double distributed parity?

  1. $\textsf{RAID 10}$
  2. $\textsf{RAID 2}$
  3. $\textsf{RAID 6}$
  4. $\textsf{RAID 5}$

The output expression of the following gate network is

  1. $\text{X.Y} + \overline{\text{X}} . \overline{\text{Y}}$
  2. $\text{X.Y + X.Y}$
  3. $\text{X.Y}$
  4. $\text{X + Y}$

The hamming distance between the octets of $\textsf{0xAA}$ and $\textsf{0x55}$ is

  1. $7$
  2. $5$
  3. $8$
  4. $6$

Consider a 32-bit machine where four-level paging scheme is used. If the hit ratio to TLB is 98%, and it takes 20 nanosecond to search the TLB and 100 nanoseconds to access the main memory what is effective memory access time in nanoseconds?

  1. 126
  2. 128
  3. 122
  4. 120

Data is transmitted continuously at $2.048$ Mbps rate for $10$ hours and received $512$ bits errors. What is the bit error rate?

  1. $\text{6.9 e-9}$
  2. $\text{6.9 e-6}$
  3. $\text{69 e-9}$
  4. $\text{4 e-9}$

Warnier Diagram enables the analyst to represent

  1. Class structure
  2. Information Hierarchy
  3. Data Flow
  4. State Transition

Given

X: 0 10 16
Y: 6 16 28

The interpolated value X=4 using piecewise linear interpolation is

  1. 11
  2. 4
  3. 22
  4. 10

In functional dependency Armstrong inference rules refers to

  1. Reflexive, Augmentation and Decomposition
  2. Transitive, Augmentation and Reflexive
  3. Augmentation, Transitive, Reflexive and Decomposition
  4. Reflexive, Transitive and Decomposition

Number of chips $(128 \times 8 \;\text{RAM})$ needed to provide a memory capacity of $2048$ bytes

  1. $2$
  2. $4$
  3. $8$
  4. $16$

There are three processes in the ready queue. When the currently running process requests for I/O how many process switches take place?

  1. 1
  2. 2
  3. 3
  4. 4

Let $T(n)$ be defined by $T(1) =10$ and $T(n+1)=2n+T(n)$ for all integers $n \geq 1$. Which of the following  represents the order of growth of $T(n)$ as a function of $n$?

  1. $O(n)$
  2. $O(n \log n)$
  3. $O(n^2)$
  4. $O(n^3)$

Which of the following UNIX command allows scheduling a program to be executed at the specifies time?

  1. cron
  2. nice
  3. date and time
  4. schedule

In DMA transfer scheme, the transfer scheme other than burst mode is

  1. cycle technique
  2. stealing technique
  3. cycle stealing technique
  4. cycle bypass technique

$n$-th derivative of $x^n$ is

  1. $nx^{n-1}$
  2. $n^n.n!$
  3. $nx^n!$
  4. $n!$

A total of 9 units of a resource type available, and given the safe state shown below, which of the following sequence will be a safe state?

Process Used Max
$P_1$ 2 7
$P_2$ 1 6
$P_3$ 2 5
$P_4$ 1 4
  1. $\langle P_4, P_1, P_3, P_2\rangle$
  2. $\langle P_4, P_2, P_1, P_3\rangle $
  3. $\langle P_4, P_2, P_3, P_1\rangle $
  4. $\langle P_3, P_1, P_2, P_4 \rangle$

Three coins are tossed simultaneously. The probability that they will fall two heads and one tail is

  1. $5/8$
  2. $1/8$
  3. $2/3$
  4. $3/8$

The average depth of a binary search tree is

  1. $O(n^{0.5})$
  2. $O(n)$
  3. $O(\log n)$
  4. $O(n \log n)$

What is the output of the following C code?

#include <stdio.h>

int main()
{
    int index;
    for(index=1; index<=5; index++)
    {
        printf("%d", index);
        if (index==3)
            continue;
    }
}
  1. 1245
  2. 12345
  3. 12245
  4. 12354

When n-type semiconductor is heated?

  1. number of electrons increases while that of holes decreases
  2. number of holes increases while that of electrons decreases
  3. number of electrons and holes remain the same
  4. number of electron and holes increases equally

The Cyclomatic Complexity metric V(G) of the following control flow graph

  1. 3
  2. 4
  3. 5
  4. 6

Which of the following algorithm design technique is used in merge sort?

  1. Greedy method
  2. Backtracking
  3. Dynamic programming
  4. Divide and Conquer

The arithmetic mean of attendance of $49$ students of class $\text{A}$ is $40%$ and that of $53$ students of class $\text{B}$ is $35%.$ Then the percentage of arithmetic mean of attendance of class $\text{A}$ and $\text{B}$ is

  1. $27.2\%$
  2. $50.25\%$
  3. $51.13\%$
  4. $37.4\%$

Which of the following sentences can be generated by 

S -> aS $\mid$ bA

A -> d $\mid$ cA

  1. bccdd
  2. abbcca
  3. abcabc
  4. abcd

Lightweight Directory Access protocol is used for

  1. Routing the packets
  2. Authentication
  3. obtaining IP address
  4. domain name resolving

Number of comparisons required for an unsuccessful search of an element in a sequential search organized, fixed length, symbol table of length L is

  1. L
  2. L/2
  3. (L+1)/2
  4. 2L

One SAN switch has $24$ ports. All $24$ supports $8$ Gbps Fiber Channel technology. What is the aggregate bandwidth of that SAN switch?

  1. $96$ Gbps
  2. $192$ Mbps
  3. $512$ Gbps
  4. $192$ Gbps

Find the output of the following Java code line 

System.out.printIn(math.floor(-7.4))
  1. -7
  2. -8
  3. -7.4
  4. -7.0

Belady's anomaly means

  1. Page fault rate is constant even on increasing the number of allocated frames
  2. Page fault rate may increase on increasing the number of allocated frames
  3. Page fault rate may increase on decreasing the number of allocated frames
  4. Page fault rate may decrease on increasing the number of allocated frames

In an $RS$ flip-flop, if the $S$ line (Set line) is set high ($1$) and the $R$ line (Reset line) is set low ($0$), then the state of the flip-flop is :

  1. Set to $1$
  2. Set to $0$
  3. No change in state
  4. Forbidden

In HTML, which of the following can be considered a container?

  1. <SELECT>
  2. <Value>
  3. <INPUT>
  4. <BODY>

What is the matrix that represents rotation of an object by $\theta^0$ about the origin in $\text{2D}?$

  1. $\cos \theta$ $- \sin \theta$
    $\sin \theta$ $\cos \theta$
  2. $\sin \theta$ $- \cos \theta$
    $\cos \theta$ $\sin \theta$
  3. $\cos \theta$ $- \sin \theta$
    $\cos \theta$ $\sin \theta$
  4. $\sin \theta$ $- \cos \theta$
    $\cos \theta$ $\sin \theta$

In a system using single processor, a new process arrives at the rate of six processes per minute and each such process requires seven seconds of service time. What is the CPU utilization?

  1. 70%
  2. 30%
  3. 60%
  4. 64%

A symbol table of length $152$ is processing $25$ entries at any instant. What is occupation density?

  1. $0.164$
  2. $127$
  3. $8.06$
  4. $6.08$

A problem whose language is recursion is called?

  1. Unified problem
  2. Boolean function
  3. Recursive problem
  4. Decidable

Logic family popular for low power dissipation

  1. CMOS
  2. ECL
  3. TTL
  4. DTL
